name: Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 14 oz nonfat yogurt (if using Dannon, it's approximately 16 oz; if not, use about 3 tablespoons less sugar)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized bowl, whip egg whites until stiff but not dry. Gradually add 1/4 cup sugar while whipping.
2. In a separate large bowl, strain yogurt if needed to remove excess liquid (straining should take no more than a few minutes). Stir in baking soda and flour, then fold in limeade concentrate.
3. Mix a small amount of egg white mixture into the yogurt mixture until well combined.
4. Fold the remaining egg white mixture into the yogurt mixture, blending gently without over-stirring to maintain fluffiness.
5. Grease a 9-inch springform pan with oil and rub it in using your fingers on the bottom, edges, and sides.
6. Pour the main batter into the prepared pan.
7. If desired, prepare the optional crust by mixing sugar, flour, and juice until well blended and pourable. Spread around the outer edge of the cheesecake mixture to form a 1/2-inch band about 1/4 inch from the pan's side.
8. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
9. Bake for approximately 25 minutes or until set. Check after 15 minutes by shaking the pan; if it jiggles, it's done. If too liquidy but browned, lower the temperature slightly.
10. Serve immediately for best texture. Refrigerating overnight will make it flatter but still delicious (serves about 6 slices).
